% ============================================================
\section{The Full Lagrangian}
\label{sec:lagrangian}
% Source: The_derivative_of_Lagernizan_SIDM/lagrangian_derivation.md
% Source: arxiv/main.tex Eq.(1)
% ============================================================

\subsection{Field Content}

\begin{center}
\begin{tabular}{llccl}
\toprule
Field & Symbol & Type & Spin & Role \\
\midrule
Dark matter & $\chi$ & Majorana fermion & $\tfrac{1}{2}$ & DM candidate ($\chi = \chi^c$) \\
Mediator & $\phi$ & Real scalar & $0$ & Self-interaction mediator ($\phi = \phi^\dagger$) \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{center}

The dark sector is \textbf{secluded}: no tree-level coupling to the Standard Model.

\subsection{Full Lagrangian}

\begin{equation}\label{eq:full_lagrangian}
\boxed{
  \mathcal{L}
  = \underbrace{\tfrac{1}{2}\bar\chi(i\!\not\!\partial - \mchi)\chi}_{\mathcal{L}_\chi}
  + \underbrace{\tfrac{1}{2}(\partial\phi)^2
    - \tfrac{1}{2}\mphi^2\phi^2
    - \tfrac{\mu_3}{3!}\phi^3
    - \tfrac{\lambda_\phi}{4!}\phi^4}_{\mathcal{L}_\phi}
  - \underbrace{\tfrac{1}{2}\bar\chi\bigl(y_s + i\,y_p\,\gamma_5\bigr)\chi\,\phi}_{\mathcal{L}_{\rm int}}
}
\end{equation}

\subsubsection{Fermion sector $\mathcal{L}_\chi$}

\begin{equation}
  \mathcal{L}_\chi = \frac{1}{2}\bar\chi(i\gamma^\mu\partial_\mu - \mchi)\chi
\end{equation}

The factor $\tfrac{1}{2}$ is the Majorana normalization (prevents double-counting of degrees of freedom).  Majorana condition: $\chi = \chi^c$ (identical to its own antiparticle), yielding $g_\chi = 2$ internal degrees of freedom.

\subsubsection{Scalar sector $\mathcal{L}_\phi$}

\begin{equation}
  \mathcal{L}_\phi
  = \frac{1}{2}\partial_\mu\phi\,\partial^\mu\phi
  - \frac{1}{2}\mphi^2\phi^2
  - \frac{\mu_3}{3!}\phi^3
  - \frac{\lambda_\phi}{4!}\phi^4
\end{equation}

\begin{center}
\begin{tabular}{ll}
\toprule
Term & Physical meaning \\
\midrule
$\tfrac{1}{2}(\partial\phi)^2$ & Kinetic energy \\
$-\tfrac{1}{2}\mphi^2\phi^2$ & Mediator mass \\
$-\tfrac{\mu_3}{3!}\phi^3$ & Cubic coupling $\to$ cannibal $3\phi\to 2\phi$ \\
$-\tfrac{\lambda_\phi}{4!}\phi^4$ & Quartic self-coupling \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{center}

\subsubsection{Yukawa interaction $\mathcal{L}_{\rm int}$}
% Paper Eq.~(1)

\begin{equation}\label{eq:lagrangian_int}
  \mathcal{L}_{\rm int}
  = -\frac{1}{2}\bar\chi\bigl(y_s + i\,y_p\,\gamma_5\bigr)\chi\,\phi
  \tag{Paper Eq.~1}
\end{equation}

\begin{itemize}
  \item $y_s$: scalar Yukawa coupling $\;\to\; \alpha_s = y_s^2/(4\pi)$
  \item $y_p$: pseudoscalar Yukawa coupling $\;\to\; \alpha_p = y_p^2/(4\pi)$
  \item Both are required: pure scalar ($y_p=0$) gives $p$-wave--suppressed annihilation.
    Simultaneous $y_s\neq 0$ and $y_p\neq 0$ breaks CP in the dark sector and restores $s$-wave.
  \item The factor $\tfrac{1}{2}$ is again the Majorana normalization.
\end{itemize}

\subsubsection{Pure-scalar limit (used in most code)}

For self-interaction calculations, $y_p$ contributes only a $(\mphi/\mchi)^2 \sim 10^{-7}$ correction to the potential and is neglected.  The effective Lagrangian used by the VPM solver is:
\begin{equation}
  \mathcal{L}_{\rm int}^{\rm eff} = -\frac{y}{2}\bar\chi\chi\,\phi
  \qquad\text{with}\quad \alpha \equiv \frac{y^2}{4\pi} = \alpha_s
\end{equation}


% ============================================================
\section{Key Formulas from the Paper}
\label{sec:formulas}
% ============================================================

\subsection{Yukawa Potential}
% Paper Eq.~(2)

In the non-relativistic limit, $t$-channel $\phi$ exchange generates:
\begin{equation}\label{eq:yukawa}
  \boxed{V(r) = -\frac{\alpha_s}{r}\,e^{-\mphi r}}
  \tag{Paper Eq.~2}
\end{equation}

Derivation: Fourier transform of the Born amplitude $\mathcal{M}\sim y^2/(|\mathbf{q}|^2 + \mphi^2)$:
\begin{equation}
  V(r) = -\int\frac{d^3\mathbf{q}}{(2\pi)^3}\,\frac{y^2}{|\mathbf{q}|^2+\mphi^2}\,e^{i\mathbf{q}\cdot\mathbf{r}}
       = -\frac{y^2}{4\pi}\frac{e^{-\mphi r}}{r}
       = -\frac{\alpha_s\,e^{-\mphi r}}{r}
\end{equation}

Note: The pseudoscalar contribution to the potential is velocity-suppressed by $(\mphi/\mchi)^2 \sim 10^{-7}$ and is negligible.

\subsection{Variable Phase Method (VPM) ODE}
% Paper Eq.~(3)

\begin{equation}\label{eq:vpm_ode}
  \boxed{
  \frac{d\delta_\ell}{dx}
  = +\frac{\lam\,e^{-x}}{\kappa\,x}
    \bigl[\hat\jmath_\ell(\kappa x)\cos\delta_\ell
          -\hat n_\ell(\kappa x)\sin\delta_\ell\bigr]^{2}
  }
  \tag{Paper Eq.~3}
\end{equation}

Positive sign = attractive potential.  Dimensionless variables:
\begin{center}
\begin{tabular}{lll}
\toprule
Symbol & Definition & Description \\
\midrule
$x$ & $\mphi\,r$ & Dimensionless radial coordinate \\
$\kappa$ & $k/\mphi = \mu\vrel/\mphi$ & Dimensionless momentum ($\mu = \mchi/2$) \\
$\lam$ & $\alpha_s\,\mchi/\mphi$ & Dimensionless coupling strength \\
$\hat\jmath_\ell(z)$ & $z\,j_\ell(z)$ & Riccati--Bessel function (1st kind) \\
$\hat n_\ell(z)$ & $-z\,y_\ell(z)$ & Riccati--Bessel function (2nd kind) \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{center}

Boundary condition: $\delta_\ell(x_{\rm min}) = 0$.\\
Integration: RK4, $x_{\rm min}$ to $x_{\rm max} = 50$--$100$, step count $4000$--$12000$ (adaptive).

\subsection{Transfer Cross Section for Identical Majorana Fermions}
% Paper Eq.~(4)

\begin{equation}\label{eq:sigma_T}
  \boxed{
  \sigma_T = \frac{2\pi}{k^{2}}\biggl[
    \sum_{\ell\;\text{even}}(2\ell+1)\sin^{2}\delta_\ell
    + 3\sum_{\ell\;\text{odd}}(2\ell+1)\sin^{2}\delta_\ell
  \biggr]
  }
  \tag{Paper Eq.~4}
\end{equation}

\textbf{Spin-statistics derivation:}
\begin{enumerate}
  \item $\chi$ is Majorana $\to$ identical fermions $\to$ total wave function antisymmetric.
  \item Spin-$\tfrac{1}{2}\otimes\tfrac{1}{2}$: singlet (symmetric, weight 1) $+$ triplet (antisymmetric, weight 3).
  \item Singlet $\times$ spatial antisymmetric $\to$ even $\ell$, weight $w_\ell = 1$.
  \item Triplet $\times$ spatial symmetric $\to$ odd $\ell$, weight $w_\ell = 3$.
  \item Factor $2\pi/k^2$ (not $4\pi/k^2$) includes the $\tfrac{1}{2}$ for identical-particle scattering.
\end{enumerate}

\textbf{Key identity:} $\sigma_T^{\rm tr} \equiv \sigma_{\rm el}$ exactly for identical Majorana fermions (verified to $10^{-12}$).

\subsection{Observable: $\sigma/m$}
% From core/README.md

\begin{equation}\label{eq:sigma_over_m}
  \frac{\sigma_T}{\mchi}\;\bigg[\frac{\text{cm}^2}{\text{g}}\bigg]
  = \frac{\sigma_T\;[\text{GeV}^{-2}] \times 3.894\times 10^{-28}\;\text{cm}^2/\text{GeV}^{-2}}
         {\mchi\;[\text{GeV}] \times 1.783\times 10^{-24}\;\text{g}/\text{GeV}}
\end{equation}

\subsection{Born Approximation}
% From lagrangian_derivation.md §4

In the weak-coupling limit $\lam = \alpha_s\mchi/\mphi \ll 1$:
\begin{equation}
  \sigma_T^{\rm Born} \approx \frac{2\pi}{k^2}\sin^2\delta_0^{\rm Born}
\end{equation}
VPM agrees with the Born formula to $<0.1\%$ in this regime.

Three scattering regimes:
\begin{center}
\begin{tabular}{lll}
\toprule
Regime & Condition & Behavior \\
\midrule
Born & $\lam\ll 1$ & Only $\ell=0$; $\sigma_T \approx \sigma_{\rm Born}$ \\
Resonant & $\lam\sim 1$--$30$ & Quasi-bound states enhance $\sigma_T$ \\
Classical & $\lam\gg 1$ & $\sigma_T \propto (\ln\lam)^2/v^2$ \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{center}

\subsection{Majorana vs.\ Dirac Spin Weights}
% Paper §5.6, Eq.~(9)

For \textbf{Majorana} (identical particles): $w_\ell^{\rm even} = 1$, $w_\ell^{\rm odd} = 3$, prefactor $2\pi/k^2$.

For \textbf{Dirac} (distinguishable particles): $w_\ell = 1$ for all $\ell$, prefactor $4\pi/k^2$.

\textbf{Triplet fraction} (Paper Eq.~9):
\begin{equation}\label{eq:Rtriplet}
  R_{\rm triplet}(v) \equiv
  \frac{3\sum_{\ell\;\text{odd}}(2\ell+1)\sin^2\delta_\ell}
       {\sigma_T^{\rm Maj}\,k^2/(2\pi)}
  \tag{Paper Eq.~9}
\end{equation}

Peaks at 82\% near $v\approx 60\kms$; produces $\sigma_T^{\rm Maj}/\sigma_T^{\rm Dir} = 2.2$ at rotation-curve scales.

\subsection{Sommerfeld Enhancement}
% Paper §6.5

Coulomb Sommerfeld factor ($\ell=0$):
\begin{equation}
  S_0 = \frac{2\pi\eta}{1 - e^{-2\pi\eta}}
  \qquad\text{with}\quad \eta = \frac{\alpha_s}{2v}
\end{equation}

Hulth\'en approximation for Yukawa:
\begin{equation}
  S = \frac{\pi/\epsilon_v \cdot \sinh(2\pi\epsilon_v/\epsilon_\phi)}
           {\cosh(2\pi\epsilon_v/\epsilon_\phi) - \cos(2\pi\sqrt{1/\epsilon_\phi - \epsilon_v^2/\epsilon_\phi^2})}
\end{equation}
where $\epsilon_v = v/(2\alpha_s)$, $\epsilon_\phi = \mphi/(\alpha_s\mchi)$.

At freeze-out ($x_{\rm fo}\approx 20$): $S_0 < 1.026$ for all benchmarks $\to$ \textbf{negligible correction}.

\subsection{Annihilation Cross Section ($s$-wave)}
% Paper Eq.~(7)

\begin{equation}\label{eq:sigma_v_ann}
  \boxed{
  \langle\sigma v\rangle_0
  = \frac{y_s^2\,y_p^2}{8\pi\,\mchi^2}
  = \frac{2\pi\,\alpha_s\,\alpha_p}{\mchi^2}
  }
  \tag{Paper Eq.~7}
\end{equation}

For the \textbf{pure-scalar limit} ($\alpha_s = \alpha_p \equiv \alpha$, i.e.\ $y_s = y_p$):
\begin{equation}
  \langle\sigma v\rangle_0 = \frac{\pi\alpha^2}{4\,\mchi^2}
\end{equation}

\subsection{Boltzmann Equation (Relic Density)}
% Paper Eq.~(8)

\begin{equation}\label{eq:boltzmann}
  \boxed{
  \frac{dY}{dx} = -\sqrt{\frac{\pi}{45}}\,
  \frac{g_{*S}}{\sqrt{g_*}}\,M_{\rm Pl}\,\mchi\,
  \frac{\langle\sigma v\rangle_0}{x^{2}}
  \bigl(Y^{2}-Y_{\rm eq}^{2}\bigr)
  }
  \tag{Paper Eq.~8}
\end{equation}

where $x = \mchi/T$, $Y = n_\chi/s$.

\textbf{Equilibrium yield} (non-relativistic):
\begin{equation}
  Y_{\rm eq} = \frac{45}{4\pi^4}\,\frac{g_\chi}{g_{*S}}\,
  \sqrt{\frac{\pi}{2}}\,x^{3/2}\,e^{-x}
  \qquad (g_\chi = 2 \text{ for Majorana})
\end{equation}

\textbf{Relic density}:
\begin{equation}
  \OmH = \frac{\mchi\,s_0\,Y_\infty}{\rho_{\rm crit}/h^2}
\end{equation}
with $s_0 = 2891.2\;\text{cm}^{-3}$, $\rho_{\rm crit}/h^2 = 1.054\times 10^{-5}\;\text{GeV/cm}^3$.

\subsection{Direct Detection (Higgs Portal)}
% Paper Eq.~(6)

\begin{equation}\label{eq:sigma_si}
  \sigma_{\rm SI} = \frac{y^2\sin^2\theta\,f_N^2\,\mu_N^2\,m_N^2}
                         {\pi\,v_{\rm EW}^2\,\mphi^4}
  \tag{Paper Eq.~6}
\end{equation}

Enhancement factor $(m_h/\mphi)^4 \approx 1.5\times 10^{16}$ for $\mphi = 11\MeV$.
LZ requires $\sin\theta < 6\times 10^{-10}$; BBN requires $\sin\theta > 2\times 10^{-5}$.
\textbf{Mutually exclusive by $\sim 3\times 10^4$} $\Rightarrow$ secluded model.

\subsection{Cannibal Annihilation}
% Paper Eq.~(5)

\begin{equation}\label{eq:cannibal}
  \langle\sigma v^2\rangle_{3\to 2}
  = \frac{25\sqrt{5}}{512\pi(4\pi)^6}\,\frac{\mu_3^6}{\mphi^{11}}
  \tag{Paper Eq.~5}
\end{equation}

Effective for $\mu_3/\mphi \gtrsim 1.7$; depletes $\phi$ population before BBN.

\subsection{Maxwell--Boltzmann Velocity Averaging}
% Paper Appendix D

\begin{equation}
  \langle\sigmam\rangle_{\rm MB}
  = \frac{\int_0^\infty (\sigmam)(v)\,v^3\,e^{-v^2/2v_0^2}\,dv}
         {\int_0^\infty v^3\,e^{-v^2/2v_0^2}\,dv}
\end{equation}

where $v_0 = v_{\rm char}/\sqrt{2}$ is the 1D velocity dispersion.

\section{Model Parameters Table}
\label{sec:parameters}
% Source: arxiv/main.tex Section 2
% ============================================================

\subsection{Lagrangian Parameters}

\begin{center}
\begin{tabular}{llll}
\toprule
Parameter & Range & Physical meaning & Sector \\
\midrule
$\mchi$ & $10$--$100\GeV$ & DM mass & Self-interaction, relic \\
$\mphi$ & $1$--$50\MeV$ & Mediator mass & Self-interaction \\
$\alpha_s = y_s^2/(4\pi)$ & $10^{-5}$--$5\times 10^{-2}$ & Scalar dark fine-structure constant & Self-interaction, relic \\
$\alpha_p = y_p^2/(4\pi)$ & --- (derived from relic) & Pseudoscalar coupling & Relic only \\
$\mu_3$ & $\gtrsim 1.7\,\mphi$ & Cubic self-coupling & Mediator cosmology only \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{center}

\textbf{Sector decoupling:}
\begin{enumerate}
  \item Self-interactions depend only on $(\mchi,\mphi,\alpha_s)$.
  \item Relic density depends on $\alpha_s\alpha_p$ (one extra constraint).
  \item Mediator cosmology depends on $\mu_3$ alone.
  \item No SM-coupling parameters.
\end{enumerate}

\end{equation}

\section{Core Code Function Signatures}
\label{sec:functions}
% ============================================================

\subsection{core/v22\_raw\_scan.py — VPM Phase-Shift Solver}

\begin{lstlisting}
# Spherical Bessel functions (Numba JIT)
def sph_jn_numba(l, z)           # j_l(z) — spherical Bessel 1st kind
def sph_yn_numba(l, z)           # y_l(z) — spherical Bessel 2nd kind

# VPM ODE right-hand side
def _vpm_rhs(l, kappa, lam, x, delta)
    # d(delta_l)/dx for Yukawa potential

# Phase shift integrator (RK4)
def vpm_phase_shift(l, kappa, lam, x_max=50.0, N_steps=4000)
    # Returns delta_l for given (l, kappa, lambda)

# Main cross-section function
def sigma_T_vpm(m_chi, m_phi, alpha, v_km_s)
    # -> sigma_T/m [cm^2/g] for identical Majorana fermions
    # Units: m_chi [GeV], m_phi [GeV], alpha [-], v_km_s [km/s]
    # Sums partial waves: w=1 (even l), w=3 (odd l)

\subsection{cross\_checks/sommerfeld.py — Sommerfeld Enhancement}

\begin{lstlisting}
def _sommerfeld_ode(kappa, lam)
    # Solve l=0 radial ODE for Yukawa potential
    # u'' + [kappa^2 + lambda*exp(-x)/x] u = 0
    # Returns S_0 = 1/(kappa^2 * A^2)

def sommerfeld_yukawa(m_chi_GeV, m_phi_GeV, alpha, v_km_s)
    # Hybrid: ODE for kappa<10, Coulomb analytic for kappa>=10
    # Returns S_0 (l=0 Sommerfeld factor)

def sommerfeld_coulomb_analytic(alpha, v_km_s)
    # S = 2*pi*eta / (1 - exp(-2*pi*eta)), eta = alpha/(2v)

def validate()   # Tests against known limits
def main()
\end{lstlisting}

\section{Observational Data}
\label{sec:observations}
% Source: data/global_config.json
% ============================================================

\begin{center}
\begin{longtable}{lcccl}
\toprule
System & $v$ [km/s] & $\sigmam$ [cm$^2$/g] & 68\% CL & Ref. \\
\midrule
\endhead
Draco dSph     &   12 & 0.6  & [0.1, 2.0]  & KTY16 \\
Fornax dSph    &   12 & 0.8  & [0.2, 3.0]  & KTY16 \\
NGC 2976       &   60 & 2.0  & [0.5, 5.0]  & KTY16 \\
NGC 1560       &   55 & 3.0  & [1.0, 8.0]  & KTY16 \\
IC 2574        &   50 & 1.5  & [0.3, 5.0]  & KTY16 \\
NGC 720 (group)&  250 & 0.5  & [0.1, 1.5]  & KTY16 \\
NGC 1332 (group)& 280 & 0.3  & [0.05, 1.0] & KTY16 \\
Abell 611      & 1200 & 0.1  & [0.02, 0.3] & KTY16 \\
Abell 2537     & 1100 & 0.15 & [0.03, 0.4] & KTY16 \\
Diverse RC band&   40 & 3.0  & [0.5, 10.0] & KKPY17 \\
Bullet Cluster$^*$& 4700& 0.7 & $<1.25$     & Randall+08 \\
72 mergers$^*$ & 1000 & 0.2  & $<0.47$     & Harvey+15 \\
TBTF dwarfs    &   30 & 1.0  & [0.5, 5.0]  & Elbert+15 \\
\bottomrule
\end{longtable}
\end{center}

$^*$One-sided upper limits ($\texttt{lo}=0$): no $\chi^2$ penalty when theory $<$ upper bound.


% ============================================================
\section{CP-Separation Band}
\label{sec:cp_band}
% Source: arxiv/main.tex §6.6, lagrangian_derivation.md §7
% ============================================================

The relic constraint fixes $\alpha_s\alpha_p = \langle\sigma v\rangle_0\,\mchi^2/(2\pi)$
while self-interactions depend only on $\alpha_s$.  This creates a one-parameter family:

\begin{center}
\begin{tabular}{lll}
\toprule
Benchmark & $\alpha_s/\alpha_p$ range & Dynamic range \\
\midrule
BP1 & $13$--$212$ & 1.22 decades \\
MAP & $1.8$--$11{,}532$ & 3.81 decades \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{center}

\textbf{CP violation is a generic prediction}, not fine-tuning.

% ============================================================
\section{Summary of Key Results}
\label{sec:summary}
% ============================================================

\begin{itemize}
  \item \textbf{3-parameter model}: $(\mchi,\mphi,\alpha_s)$ fully specifies SIDM phenomenology.
  \item \textbf{80,142 viable points} from raw scan; \textbf{17 relic-constrained benchmarks}.
  \item \textbf{Best $\chi^2$ fit}: $\chi^2/\nu = 0.12$ (unconstrained), $0.38$ (relic-constrained) to 13 systems.
  \item \textbf{Secluded}: $\sigma_{\rm SI} = \sigma_{\rm SD} = 0$ exactly. Higgs portal excluded by $3\times 10^4$.
  \item \textbf{Majorana fingerprint}: $\sigma_T^{\rm Maj}/\sigma_T^{\rm Dir} = 2.2$ at $v\approx 60\kms$.
  \item \textbf{Triplet dominance}: CP-odd channel carries 82\% at rotation-curve velocities.
  \item \textbf{Sommerfeld}: $S_0 < 1.026$ at freeze-out $\to$ tree-level relic density valid.
  \item \textbf{BSF}: $\sigma v_{\rm BSF}/\langle\sigma v\rangle_{\rm ann} \sim 10^{-12}$ $\to$ negligible.
  \item \textbf{SMBH seeding}: unambiguously negative ($t_{\rm gc}/t_{\rm universe} > 10^4$).
\end{itemize}

\end{document}
